SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.optimization.valuechain

Class DebottleneckingAdvisor

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.optimization.valuechain.DebottleneckingAdvisor
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class DebottleneckingAdvisor extends Object implements Serializable
Ranks candidate debottlenecking investments by their incremental net present value.

Once a BottleneckTracker migration trajectory has revealed which constraint binds the plant and when, the economic question becomes which upgrade is worth making, and in which year. The advisor answers that by discounting the incremental value each candidate upgrade unlocks (extra export, deferred decline) against its capital cost, producing a ranked shortlist with NPV, benefit-cost ratio and simple payback. It is the economic companion to the physical bottleneck analysis.

The advisor is a pure ranking engine: the incremental annual value of each candidate is supplied by the caller (typically obtained by relaxing the binding constraint in NeqSim, re-running, and pricing the extra production with ValueChainObjective). This keeps the advisor fast and deterministic. When a candidate carries a live CapacityConstraint, applyShadowPrices() writes the per-year incremental value back onto the constraint as its shadow price.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

  • Constructor Details

    • DebottleneckingAdvisor

      public DebottleneckingAdvisor(EconomicParameters econ)
      Creates a debottlenecking advisor.
      Parameters:
      econ - the economic parameters supplying the discount rate (must not be null)

  • Method Details

    • addCandidate

      public DebottleneckingAdvisor addCandidate(DebottleneckingAdvisor.DebottleneckCandidate candidate)
      Adds a candidate upgrade to be evaluated.
      Parameters:
      candidate - the candidate to add (must not be null)
      Returns:
      this advisor for method chaining

    • getCandidates

      public List<DebottleneckingAdvisor.DebottleneckCandidate> getCandidates()
      Gets the candidates currently registered with the advisor.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable list of candidates

    • evaluate

      public List<DebottleneckingAdvisor.Recommendation> evaluate()
      Evaluates and ranks all candidate upgrades by net present value, highest first.

      For each candidate the present value of the incremental annual cash flow is discounted across its active years and the capital cost is discounted to its installation year. Net present value is the difference. Ties are broken by benefit-cost ratio.

      Returns:
      a list of recommendations sorted by descending net present value

    • computePayback

      private double computePayback(DebottleneckingAdvisor.DebottleneckCandidate c)
      Computes the simple (undiscounted) payback of a candidate in years from its installation year.
      Parameters:
      c - the candidate
      Returns:
      the payback in years, or positive infinity if the benefits never cover the capex

    • applyShadowPrices

      public int applyShadowPrices()
      Writes each candidate's incremental annual value back onto its live constraint as a shadow price.

      Candidates without a live CapacityConstraint are skipped. After this call the binding constraints carry the marginal value of relaxing them, queryable via CapacityConstraint.getShadowPrice().

      Returns:
      the number of constraints updated

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Renders the ranked recommendations as a JSON array.
      Returns:
      a JSON string describing the ranked recommendations

    • fmt

      private static String fmt(double value)
      Formats a double for JSON output, mapping non-finite values to null.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to format
      Returns:
      the formatted string or null

    • esc

      private static String esc(String value)
      Escapes a string for inclusion in JSON output.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to escape (may be null)
      Returns:
      the escaped string, or an empty string if null
